name: Stephen Magare Ogaro
about: Stephen is the CEO and Full-Stack Developer at Maogast Softworks (https://maogastsoftworks.com), where he leads initiatives in custom software development, printing, branding, and AI-powered design. He also serves as a Technical Lead at NK-Organics Medical Missionaries (https://nkmm.co.ke/), blending software development with content strategy, digital marketing, and natural health education. As Departmental Head for Revival & Reformation at Zimmerman SDA Church, he coordinates prayer cells, leads weekly intercessory prayer sessions, and facilitates morning devotions. A passionate servant leader, Stephen believes in using every gift—technical, spiritual, and creative—to bring health and hope through God's natural remedies. He is currently pursuing the Master Guide certification to deepen his leadership skills and better serve the youth and children in his community.
mission: “I believe in servant leadership that empowers others through mentorship, strategic vision that transforms communities, and faithful stewardship that leverages both spiritual gifts and professional skills.”
dedication: To my Heavenly Father & Lord Jesus Christ, who called me by name; to my family who supports me; and to the youth of Zimmerman SDA Church—you are why I serve.

items.title: My Journey to Master Guide: A Call to Servant Leadership
items.content:
**What it means to be a Master Guide:**

Being a Master Guide is far more than a title, a badge, or a course completion certificate. It is a sacred commission. It means stepping into the role of a spiritual parent, mentor, and navigator for the next generation. A Master Guide is a leader who actively develops the skills to teach, protect, and inspire children and teens. It involves mastering practical leadership, outdoor survival, nature appreciation, and above all, deep biblical instruction. It means representing a standard of moral integrity, faith, and service that young people can look up to and emulate in a world that constantly offers them counterfeits.

**Why I want to become one:**

My deepest motivation to pursue this calling is rooted in the urgent reality of our time. We live in a generation saturated with digital noise, moral relativity, and spiritual confusion. My primary reason is to **counteract the world’s influence** on this generation. I am called to stand in the gap and train children and young people to be firm in Christian principles—to equip them with a compass that never fails, no matter how dark the cultural storm becomes.

Additionally, I want to raise up young souls to reflect the character of our biblical patriarchs and matriarchs. I want them to possess the unwavering courage of Daniel when faced with a lion's den, the unshakable faith of Esther when her people were threatened, and the steadfast loyalty of Ruth when everything around her fell apart. I want them to know that true strength doesn't come from worldly power, but from bowing the knee to the Almighty.

Finally, I want to **build a firm foundation** and pass on a legacy. I being mentored by faithful individuals who are investing in me. Now, I feel a solemn duty to pour that same grace into the lives of the youth. By teaching them God's principles early, we ensure they don't just survive the end times—they thrive and become leaders who will carry the Adventist torch into the next generation. My ultimate ambition is to see these young people standing safely in the Kingdom of God. That is my highest goal, and that is why I am a Master Guide in training.

items.title: Academic & Professional Journey
items.description: Bachelor in Information Technology (Technical University of Kenya, 2018–2024) with honors. Software developer and prompt engineer specializing in Next.js, React, and Java. Continuous certifications and self‑directed learning in modern web technologies. Full details, including secondary and primary education, certifications, and projects, are available on my main portfolio.

items.title: Serepta Children's Home Visit – GYD 2026
items.description: On 21st March 2026, as part of Global Youth Day, I joined fellow Master Guides and youth at Serepta Children's Home. I had the joy of teaching the children, drawing with them, serving meals, and dancing together. It was a beautiful day of giving and receiving love.
